page: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dpsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an ARM64 architecture featuring a dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU configuration allocates one thread per core and includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 128 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 10% tier. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 300.63 GB/sec for read-only operations and 111.74 GB/sec for write operations. In processing tasks, single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both register in the top 25% tier. Most other compute metrics, including Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, stress-ng CPU performance, and Redis SET operations, fall within the average range. Large-model LLM inference prompt processing represents a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16ps_v6 offers a balanced resource profile for ARM-native deployments, delivering high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores without the added cost of bundled local storage or specialized GPU hardware. This makes it highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, application servers, static web serving, database operations, and software compilation tasks optimized for the ARM64 architecture.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v6 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine powered by a dedicated 16-core Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio of 4.0 GB per core. While the instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks reveal exceptional memory capabilities, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and strong large-block memory bandwidth. Compute performance is generally average, though single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression perform in the top 25%. Given its high memory bandwidth and dedicated ARM cores, the D16ps_v6 is well-suited for ARM-native applications, web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory performance, making it ideal for general-purpose ARM-native workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 16 dedicated cores, 64 GB RAM, and high-bandwidth memory for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine delivering dedicated sixteen-core processing and high-bandwidth memory for balanced general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated ARM64 architecture with 16 Microsoft Neoverse-N2 cores at 3.4 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory performance with high-bandwidth read and write capabilities
No bundled local storage or GPUs, optimized for external Premium Storage
Best suited for ARM-native applications, web serving, and database operations
